reasoning: I will mark the name Shantanu Thakur, though my hand shakes when I do it. In 2019 I believed—for the first time in fifty years, Delhi said 'Matua.' That meant something. They broke that promise with fourteen certificates for lakhs of us, yes. But TMC? They send Mamatabala to sit in Delhi and call herself our voice—this is the same game, just new faces. The Left never saw us at all. I have waited fifty years. I will vote for the one who at least named me once, even if he lied. Maybe he lies again. But the others? They offer nothing but the same silence our grandfathers knew. My wife was right to doubt me. But I cannot vote for doubt itself. I vote for the last ember of hope I have, even though I know it will go out.
primary_drivers: BJP is the only party that ever said 'Matua' and meant to deliver—even though they failed, no alternative has given more
Bitter refusal to legitimize AITC co-optation (Mamatabala) or Left indifference through a vote
Fifty years of waiting makes him vote despite knowing it is futile—inertia of hope mixed with defiance
